calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2019 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiff'S Claim For Punitive Damages [Ccp Sec 473c]
Off Calendar Jun-20-2019 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-15-2019 Continued To Aug-19-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-01-2018 Set For Trial On Apr-02-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-24-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants Dilip Misra and Monica Gandhi's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Sophom Preap is denied. Ms. Preap adequately alleges facts showing malice for purposes of punitive damages liability. The allegations that Mr. Misra intentionally attempted to flee the scene of the accident knowing that he had hit and injured Ms. Preap which caused Ms. Preap to suffer additional fear, distress and anxiety are sufficient to show despicable conduct carried on with willful and conscious disregard for Ms. Preap's rights. Defendants' reliance on Broooks v. E.J. Wilig Truck Transp. Co. (1953) 40 Cal. 2d 669 is misplaced. Nothing in that case precludes punitive damages liability based on the previously mentioned allegations. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
